etymology.description: 问(問) (wèn, "ask") refers to the act of inquiring or posing a question. 题(題) (tí, "topic; subject; title") denotes the subject or main point of discussion or inquiry. Combining these characters, 问题(問題) (wèntí, "problem; question") signifies an issue or topic that requires one to ask questions or seek solutions.
etymology.interpretation: The word 问题(問題) (wèntí) represents an inquiry (问/問) about a specific subject (题/題), making it relevant for both questions posed in an academic or conversational setting and problems that require resolution.

examples.chinese: 他提出了一个问题(他提出了一個問題)。
我们需要解决问题(我們需要解決問題)。
examples.english: We need to solve the problem.

examples.chinese: 如果有问题(如果有問題)，请随时告诉我(請隨時告訴我)。
examples.pinyin: Rúguǒ yǒu wèntí, qǐng suíshí gàosù wǒ.
examples.english: If there are any problems, please let me know at any time.
